Launch the stdio subprocess inside the code_sandbox bwrap
 isolation. Honored for any `transport_type == Stdio` row (both
 system and user-owned) — the user-create handler force-sets this
 to `true` for user-owned stdio servers per the active MCP user
 policy; admins choose freely on system servers via the drawer
 toggle. The spawn path ignores it for non-stdio servers.

sandbox_flavor.description:
Rootfs flavor (KNOWN_FLAVORS, e.g. `minimal`/`full`) used when
 `run_in_sandbox` launches this stdio server inside the
 code_sandbox. Defaults to `full` (the flavor that ships Node +
 uv + python3 + R). Ignored when not sandboxed. For user-owned
 stdio rows the user-create handler force-overwrites this with
 the active `mcp_user_policy.user_stdio_sandbox_flavor` (the
 drawer hides the picker on the user side); admins choose it on
 the system drawer.

McpToolCall.description:
One recorded MCP tool-call invocation (a `mcp_tool_calls` row).

 `source`/`status` are stored (and surfaced) as their snake_case strings.
 Field ORDER must match the SELECT/RETURNING column list in `repository.rs`
 (`query_as!` maps by name, but keeping them aligned aids review).

McpTransport.description:
Transport block on an `McpPackage`. The official `server.json`
 schema models this as an `anyOf` over three single-field structs
 (`{type: "stdio"}`, `{type: "streamable-http"}`, `{type: "sse"}`).
 We flatten that to one struct with a single `type` field — round-
 trips all three shapes cleanly.

type.description:
`"stdio"` | `"streamable-http"` | `"sse"`. Consumer doesn't
 read this today; ziee install path implies stdio whenever a
 `package[]` entry is selected.

McpUserPolicy.description:
The global MCP user-policy row (singleton; id always = 1 in the DB).

 Drives what regular users can install:
   - `allowed_transports == []` → no Add button, no Hub MCP tab for
     non-admins.
   - `'stdio' ∈ allowed_transports` → user-installed stdio MCP rows
     get `run_in_sandbox = true` + `sandbox_flavor =
     user_stdio_sandbox_flavor` force-applied at create/update time.

 Admin-only edits via PUT /api/mcp/user-policy (perm
 `McpUserPolicyEdit`); read is open to any authenticated user (the
 UI needs it to gate the Add button + Hub tab visibility).

allowed_transports.description:
Subset of `{"http", "stdio"}`. `sse` is admin-only (not
 exposed in the user-mode drawer dropdown).

tool_call_retention_days.description:
Days to retain MCP tool-call history (`mcp_tool_calls`) before the
 background prune deletes it. `0` = keep forever (no prune).

user_stdio_sandbox_flavor.description:
Required iff `"stdio" ∈ allowed_transports`; must be in
 `code_sandbox::types::KNOWN_FLAVORS`.

has_password.description:
Whether this account has a local password set (`password_hash IS
 NOT NULL`). `password_hash` itself is never serialized, so this
 derived flag is how the client decides whether to offer a
 self-service "change password" form (false for OAuth/LDAP-only).

permissions.description: Effective permissions (union of user's direct permissions + all active group permissions)

MemoryAdminSettings.description: Deployment-wide memory admin settings (single row, id=1).

daily_extraction_quota.description: Per-user/day extraction quota (rows created via extraction).

fts_candidate_multiplier.description:
Hybrid retrieval pulls top-K × this many candidates from each arm
 before RRF fusion. Higher = more recall, more DB load.

fts_dictionary.description:
Postgres dictionary used by `websearch_to_tsquery` at retrieval
 time AND in the GENERATED expression on `user_memories.content_tsv`.
 `simple` = no stemming, language-agnostic. `english`/`spanish`/etc.
 = Porter stemmer for that language only. Changing this requires
 the explicit rebuild flow (`POST /memory/admin/fts/rebuild`); the
 PUT settings handler returns 409 on a dictionary change.

fts_enabled.description:
FTS arm kill switch. When `false`, the retriever skips FTS even
 in the no-embedding-model path (then it bails entirely).

fts_min_rank.description:
`ts_rank_cd` cutoff. 0.0 = no filter (default). Increase to drop
 weak lexical matches at query time.

fts_rebuild_completed_at.description: Set when the most recent FTS rebuild finished successfully.

fts_rebuild_started_at.description:
Set when an FTS rebuild starts; cleared when it completes (or
 stays NULL if no rebuild has ever run on this deployment).

fts_rrf_k.description:
Reciprocal Rank Fusion constant for hybrid (vector ⊕ FTS) recall.
 Higher k = more egalitarian; lower = lopsided toward each arm's
 top-ranked. Default 60 matches the RRF paper.

semantic_enabled.description:
Semantic (vector) arm kill switch. When `false`, the retriever
 skips the vector arm regardless of whether an embedding model is
 configured. Effective vector recall requires
 `semantic_enabled AND embedding_model_id IS NOT NULL`.

soft_delete_grace_days.description: Reaper hard-delete grace period for soft-deleted memories (days).

MemoryAuditEntry.description: Append-only audit entry for memory operations.

MemoryListResponse.description:
Paginated response shape for the per-user memory list — matches
 the convention used by `McpServerListResponse` /
 `LlmRepositoryListResponse`, so the UI can drive a standard
 antd `<Pagination>` from the response (current_page, total).

Message.description:
Message entity - Represents a single message in a conversation
 Messages belong to branches via the branch_messages junction table

assistant_id.description:
`None` when the message exists (and the caller owns it) but
 was sent WITHOUT an assistant. `Some(uuid)` when an assistant
 was attributed at send-time.

MessageContent.description: Message content entity - Represents a content block within a message

MessageContentData.description:
Content data types - All content types come from extensions via composition

 Extensions add variants by defining MessageContentDataVariants enums in their
 extension.rs files. The compose_message_content_variants macro merges them at compile time.

oneOf.description: Plain text content
Thinking/reasoning content (Claude-style extended thinking)
Image content with source
File attachment content

version_id.description:
The exact version this block pins (the head at send time). `None` on
 legacy blocks → the renderer follows the current head.

oneOf.description: Tool use request (AI wants to call a tool)

name.description: Tool name (without server_id prefix)

oneOf.description: Tool result (response from tool execution)

attachment.description:
Inline file attachment returned by a tool (base64-encoded content).
 Mirrors `McpContentData::ToolResult.attachment` so the
 `to_message_content()` serde roundtrip in content.rs preserves
 it through DB persistence. Without this field here, serde
 silently drops it when re-deserialising into MessageContentData.

hidden_content.description:
System context for the LLM (e.g. download URLs) — never rendered to users.
 Stripped from API responses by strip_hidden_content_serialize in core/models/content.rs.

images.description:
Inline images returned by a tool (base64). Mirrors
 `McpContentData::ToolResult.images`; replayed to the model as image
 blocks. Same serde-roundtrip requirement as `attachment` above.

name.description: Function/tool name (required for some providers like Gemini)

resource_links.description:
References to persisted files returned by a tool (MCP
 resource_link). MUST be persisted on the assistant message —
 the frontend's `MessageFilesView` keys inline file-preview
 rendering off this field. Missing-from-schema means missing-
 from-storage means no inline preview.

server_id.description: ID of the MCP server that executed this tool (UUID string)

structured_content.description:
The MCP tool response's `structuredContent`, persisted verbatim. Mirrors
 `McpContentData::ToolResult.structured_content` so the `to_message_content()`
 serde roundtrip preserves it through DB persistence (same requirement as
 `attachment`/`resource_links` above). UI-only — content-type renderers (e.g.
 the literature screening card) read it, and the model recalls it via
 `get_tool_result`; it is NOT forwarded to the LLM by `to_content_block`.

oneOf.description:
Elicitation request — MCP server asking the user for structured input.
 Persisted to DB immediately when the request arrives so it survives page reloads.

elicitation_id.description: Per-elicitation random UUID matching the SSE event

message.description: Human-readable prompt from the MCP server

requested_schema.description: JSON Schema (SEP-1330) describing the requested fields

response_content.description: User's submitted field values (only present when status = "accepted")

server.description: Display name of the MCP server that sent the request

MessageHistoryQuery.description:
Query params for `GET /conversations/{id}/messages`.

 The cursor is a **message_id** (resolved server-side to its
 `branch_messages.created_at` on the conversation's ACTIVE branch). At most
 ONE of `before` / `after` / `around` may be set:
 - none    → the newest `limit` messages (tail),
 - `before`→ the `limit` messages immediately OLDER than the cursor,
 - `after` → the `limit` messages immediately NEWER than the cursor,
 - `around`→ a window CENTERED on the cursor (≈limit/2 older + it + ≈limit/2 newer).

ordinal.description: 1-based GLOBAL position within the full match set (stable across pages).

snippet.description: A bounded, ellipsized excerpt of the matching text around the hit.

q.description: Case-insensitive substring term. Blank/whitespace → empty result.

MessageSearchResults.description: A page of in-conversation search results.

total.description: Full match count across all pages (drives the "X of Y" readout).

MistralRsCommand.description: MistralRS command types for different model formats and use cases

oneOf.description: Plain model format (safetensors/pytorch)
GGUF quantized model format
Auto-loader for various model formats
Vision-enabled plain models for multimodal capabilities
X-LoRA (Cross-Layer LoRA) models
LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) models
TOML configuration-based models

MistralRsSettings.description: MistralRs-specific settings for individual model performance and batching configuration

chat.description: Chat capability - can engage in conversational text generation

context_length.description:
Native max context window (tokens). Parsed from GGUF / safetensors for
 local models and serialized into the capabilities JSONB by the
 llm_local_runtime validator; surfaced here so the UI can show the ceiling
 and the summarizer can use the effective window. (B6a)

image_generator.description: Image generation capability - can generate images from text descriptions

text_embedding.description: Text embedding capability - can generate text embeddings for semantic search

tools.description: Tools capability - can use function calling/tools

ModelFromHubResponse.description: Response for model download initiated from hub

repeat_last_n.description: Number of last tokens to consider for repetition penalty

ModelQuantization.description:
One quantization choice nested under a `ModelSource`. Captures the
 download-time selection a user makes in the FE (Q4_K_M vs Q5_K_M
 vs f16, etc.).

fileSha256.description:
Optional sha256 (hex) for integrity verification. Not enforced
 today; kept on the wire for forward compat.

isDefault.description:
Marks the install-time default. Exactly one entry SHOULD set
 this; the install handler falls back to `quantizations[0]` if
 no entry does.

mainFile.description:
File within the source repo to download (matches v1's
 `main_filename` semantics — passed straight through to the
 download path).

sizeGb.description:
Size in GB (informational; surfaced in the FE). Optional —
 publishers commonly omit it for non-default quants where the
 canonical size is the default's. The FE renders "—" when absent.

ModelShape.description:
The kind of weight container a repo holds. Priority gguf > safetensors >
 pickle mirrors how the engine decides what to load.

ModelSource.description:
One installable source variant under `HubModel.sources`. Parallel to
 MCP's `McpPackage`: each source pins a registry, identifier, version,
 and (model-specific) one or more `quantizations[]`.

contextLength.description: Per-source context length (replaces v1 model-wide field).

environmentVariables.description:
Env vars the source needs (e.g. `HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY`). Reuses
 the same `McpKeyValueInput` struct used by `McpPackage` /
 `McpRemote`. The install handler's auth gate iterates this list
 to find required+secret entries.

fileFormat.description: Per-source file format. Replaces the v1 model-wide `file_format`.

identifier.description:
Repo path (`huggingface`/`s3`) or absolute URL (`url`). For
 `huggingface` this is `owner/repo`.

quantizations.description:
Per-source quantization choices. At least one entry per source
 (the publisher always lists the default file as a single
 quantization). `is_default: true` marks the install-time default.

registryType.description:
`"huggingface"` | `"s3"` | `"url"` | `"local"` etc. Drives URL
 derivation in the install handler.

runtimeHint.description:
`"mistralrs"` | `"llamacpp"` etc. Informational; the install
 path does not currently use this to pick an engine.

ModelUsageInfo.description: A local model and how it relates to a given engine version.

pinned.description:
True if the model is explicitly pinned to this version
 (`required_runtime_version_id`); false if it merely inherits it via
 the provider/system default.

running.description: Whether a runtime instance is currently running for this model.

MountEntry.description: One mounted host folder (stored as a JSONB array element on `host_mounts`).

host_path.description: Absolute path on the user's machine (the desktop host).

read_only.description:
Read-only by default; read-write additionally requires the policy's
 `allow_readwrite`.

interrupt.description: TRUE => client may toast on arrival; FALSE => durable inbox row only.

OnboardingProgress.description:
Per-user onboarding progress. Step ids use the composite
 "{guide_id}/{step_id}" key format. Replaces the two columns that
 previously rode on the `User` object.

PaginatedMessages.description:
A page of messages from the active branch, chronological ascending. Cursors
 are the window endpoints — the client sends `messages[0].id` as the next
 `before` (scroll-up) and `messages[last].id` as the next `after` (scroll-down).

PaginationQuery.description:
Pagination query that clamps at deserialize time so every existing
 handler that consumes `params.page` and `params.per_page` is safe
 without touching its body.

 - `page < 1` → 1 (prevents `(page-1)*per_page` underflow / negative offset)
 - `per_page < 1` → 1 (prevents divide-by-zero in callers like
   `total / per_page`)
 - `per_page > PAGINATION_MAX_PER_PAGE` → PAGINATION_MAX_PER_PAGE
   (prevents DoS)

limit.description: Items per page; clamped to [1, ASSISTANT_MAX_LIMIT] at deserialize.

page.description: Page number (1-indexed); clamped to ≥1 at deserialize.

PaginationQuery4.description:
Pagination params for project list endpoints. Both `page` and
 `limit` are **optional** in the wire schema (defaults: page=1,
 limit=20). The custom `Deserialize` clamps values into [1, 100]
 silently so callers can't cause unbounded materialization.

 The fields are `Option<i64>` in the schema-visible layout so the
 generated OpenAPI marks them `required: false` (closes audit N15).
 The handler bodies call `.resolved()` to get clamped i64s.

limit.description: Items per page. Defaults to 20, clamped to [1, 100].

search.description:
Optional case-insensitive search term. Matches a conversation's title
 OR the text of any of its messages (substring). Omit for no filter.

sort.description:
Optional sort order: `recent` (default, most-recently updated first),
 `oldest`, `alpha` (by title A→Z), or `most_messages`. Unknown values
 fall back to `recent`.

PasswordOnlyLoginRequest.description:
Body of `POST /api/auth/login-password-only`. Authenticates as the
 single admin user using just a password (no username). Used by
 the remote-served login page when `hide_username: true`.

max_iteration.description: Maximum number of times this tool can be called per conversation turn

ProbeFailure.description:
Structured probe failure carrying the underlying reason so the
 caller can surface it (in the API response, in the boot log, or
 in the UI toast).

reason.description:
Human-readable reason — taken verbatim from
 `test_repository_connectivity`'s `Err(String)` (timeout / 401 /
 DNS / etc.).

ProbeFailure2.description:
Structured probe failure carrying the underlying reason so the
 caller can surface it (in the API response, in the boot log, or
 in the UI toast).

reason.description:
Human-readable reason — taken verbatim from
 `TestMcpConnectionResponse.message` (timeout / 401 / bad
 command / etc.).

ProgressKind.description:
The typed `progress.v1` payload of one live track (P2.2/P2.3). The author
 writes this FLAT (`{ "type":"bar", "fraction":0.4 }`); the sandbox-progress
 parser maps it into this nested form (kind under `kind`). All strings are
 plaintext (the FE renders them escaped).

ProgressTrack.description:
One live progress track inside a sandbox step. `id` keys parallel substeps
 (empty string = the step's single/default track); `done` finalizes/removes
 it. Persisted in `step_progress_json` (the running step's track map) and
 streamed on `StepProgress`.

Project.description:
Project entity. One row per personal project.

 MCP defaults previously lived inline (mcp_*); they moved to the
 unified `mcp_settings` table (migration 78) owned by the mcp module.
 Clients fetch them via `GET /api/projects/{id}/mcp-settings` (still
 mounted at the same URL — the route now lives in mcp's
 project_extension).

ProjectFileListResponse.description:
Response for `GET /api/projects/{id}/files` — joined with the `files`
 table so the client gets file metadata without a per-file lookup.

ProjectListQuery.description:
Query params for `GET /projects`: pagination + optional name/description
 search. A DEDICATED type (not the shared `PaginationQuery`) so the `search`
 param appears only on this endpoint's OpenAPI — mirrors the per-endpoint
 query-struct convention in `mcp/handlers/user.rs` (blind-audit FIX-A).

limit.description: Items per page. Defaults to 20, clamped to [1, 100].

search.description:
Case-insensitive substring filter on project name/description.
 Blank/whitespace-only is treated as "no filter".

ProjectMcpSettingsRequest.description:
PUT body + GET response for `/api/projects/{id}/mcp-settings`.

 Field shapes match the legacy `UpdateProjectMcpSettingsRequest` so
 the OpenAPI schema (and therefore the autogenerated TS client) is
 wire-compatible. The naming is now consistent with mcp's other
 settings types (typed enum/structs instead of raw strings/Value).

loop_settings.description:
Optional — None preserves the existing row's loop_settings.
 `LoopSettings` is fully typed (matches chat_extension's
 settings shape).

ProjectMcpSettingsResponse.description:
GET response shape. Wraps an `McpSettings` row in the same wire
 format as the legacy `UpdateProjectMcpSettingsRequest` so the
 frontend's autogen client stays compatible.

Prompt.description:
MCP Prompt template metadata (per MCP spec § server/prompts).
 Returned by `prompts/list`.

ProviderCatalogEntry.description:
One entry in the provider catalog (descriptor + current configured state).
 The API key is NEVER returned — only `api_key_set`.

api_key_set.description: True when an API key is stored (the value itself is never exposed).

config.description: Non-secret stored config for this provider.

configured.description: True when required config + (if needed) API key are present.

ProviderWithModels.description: Provider with its available models and user-facing key status

api_key.description:
API key for upstream provider — write-only.

 Deserialize unchanged so admins can POST/PUT the value; Serialize
 suppressed so GET /llm-providers and /llm-providers/{id} no longer
 return the secret. The previous behavior round-tripped the api_key
 to every user with `llm_providers::read` — closes
 06-llm-provider F-01 (Critical). The deeper at-rest encryption
 (pgcrypto / SecretView) is the follow-up A5-full work.

api_key_configured.description: Whether an API key is configured (either system-level or user-level)

default_runtime_version_id.description: Default runtime version for local models in this provider

api_key.description:
API key for upstream provider — write-only.

 Deserialize unchanged so admins can POST/PUT the value; Serialize
 suppressed so GET /llm-providers and /llm-providers/{id} no longer
 return the secret. The previous behavior round-tripped the api_key
 to every user with `llm_providers::read` — closes
 06-llm-provider F-01 (Critical). The deeper at-rest encryption
 (pgcrypto / SecretView) is the follow-up A5-full work.

default_runtime_version_id.description: Default runtime version for local models in this provider

password.description:
Proxy password — write-only.

 Accepted from request bodies (Deserialize) so admins can set the
 value, but NEVER serialized into a response. Without this guard
 the field round-tripped verbatim on every GET /llm-providers/{id}
 and /llm-providers, exposing the proxy credential. Closes
 06-llm-provider F-05 (Medium).

PublicProvider.description:
Public-safe summary of an enabled auth provider — what the login
 page needs to render a provider button. NEVER includes client_id,
 client_secret, or any other config.

in_progress.description: True while a rebuild worker holds the process-local lock.

model_name.description: Name of the currently-configured embedding model, if any.

pending_count.description:
Live rows still needing (re)embedding under the current model:
 embedding IS NULL OR embedding_model != current_model.name.
 Returns 0 if no embedding model is configured.

refresh_token.description:
The refresh token. Optional: web clients omit it and the server
 reads the httpOnly `ziee_refresh` cookie instead; desktop/tunnel
 clients keep sending it in the body. An explicit body token wins
 over the cookie, and the response mirrors the source (body-in→
 body-out, cookie-in→cookie-out).

RemoteAccessSettingsResponse.description:
GET /api/remote-access/settings response. Token is NEVER included
 in the response body — only the `auth_token_set` boolean. The
 domain IS included because it becomes public the moment the tunnel
 connects.

RemoteAccessStatusResponse.description:
GET /api/remote-access/status — combined status surface for the
 admin page. Aggregates settings + live tunnel state + password
 rotation status.

RepositoryAuthConfig.description:
Authentication configuration for LLM repositories
 This is part of the database entity and stored as JSONB in PostgreSQL.

 SECURITY: api_key / password / token are write-only — accepted from
 request bodies (Deserialize unchanged) so admins can set values, but
 NEVER returned in responses. Without these `skip_serializing` guards
 the credentials round-tripped on every GET /llm-repositories and
 /llm-repositories/{id}, exposing the secret to every user with
 `llm_repositories::read` (no per-user scope on llm_repositories).
 Closes 09-llm-repository F-02 (High). The deeper at-rest encryption
 (pgcrypto / SecretView) is the follow-up A5-full work.

suggested_main_filename.description: A sensible default to pre-fill the download form's main filename.

truncated.description: True when the upstream listing was capped (very large GitHub trees).

path.description: Repo path within the source, e.g. "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct".

ResourceLink.description:
A reference to a resource returned by a tool (MCP resource_link content type)

 Used when a tool creates or references a file that is already persisted on the server.
 The URI follows the pattern `/api/files/{file_id}` for files stored in the file system.

file_id.description:
File id of the server-saved artifact backing this link, when the backend
 persisted it as a `File` (workspace artifacts and user attachments). Lets
 the UI fetch the content via the authenticated `/api/files/{id}/...`
 endpoints (the same path the right-side panel uses) instead of
 dereferencing the raw `uri`. `None` for external MCP links with no
 backing File.

is_saved.description:
Whether the file is already persisted in originals storage (user-attached).
 true  → URI is a download-with-token URL; skip fetch-and-save pipeline.
 false → workspace file; run full processing pipeline.
 None  → external MCP server; run full processing pipeline.

name.description: Display name for the resource

version_id.description:
The exact `file_versions.id` this link pins, when the backing File is
 versioned (e.g. a sandbox version-back). Lets the UI render the precise
 version produced this turn. `None` → follow head.

content.description: Field values — only present when action == "accept"

version.description: The version number to restore (a new head is appended with its bytes).

RichFile.description: A file attachment returned by a tool (inline base64 content)

RootfsArtifact.description:
One downloaded artifact in `code_sandbox_rootfs_artifacts`.
 Field order mirrors the SQL column order so the sqlx `FromRow`
 derive matches without explicit `column()` annotations.

RootfsRelease.description:
One semver tag visible on GitHub. The version-manager surfaces these
 in the admin UI's "available" list, distinct from the "installed"
 list which comes from the DB.

asset_names.description:
Raw asset names attached to the release — useful for the UI to
 surface "only x86_64 published" cases before the admin clicks
 "Install".

version.description: Semver string with the leading `v` stripped (`"0.1.0"`).

RotateProxyTokenResponse.description:
Response from `POST /llm-providers/{id}/rotate-proxy-token`.
 Carries the new plaintext token in the same shape as create.

plaintext_api_key.description:
New plaintext token. Caller should copy + store; only this
 response carries it.

RunActionAck.description:
Acknowledgement for an in-flight-run action (cancel / set-timeout). `status`
 is the action-specific outcome string ("cancelled" / "updated" /
 "already_terminal"); shared intentionally by both endpoints.

RuntimeVersionListResponse.description: Response containing a list of runtime versions

RuntimeVersionResponse.description: Response containing a single runtime version

SSEChatStreamArtifactCreatedData.description: Event data for an artifact file created by a tool (via MCP resource_link)

file_id.description: UUID of the file in the files table

tool_use_id.description:
Tool use id this artifact was produced by. Lets the frontend attach the
 artifact's resource_link to the matching tool_result block (positioning),
 and disambiguate when tools run in parallel.

SSEChatStreamCompleteData.description: Data for the Complete SSE event

SSEChatStreamErrorData.description: Data for the Error SSE event

SSEChatStreamEvent.description:
SSE event enum for chat streaming

 This enum represents all possible Server-Sent Events that can be streamed
 during a chat message request.

 # Extension Architecture

 **EXTENSIONS SHOULD send their own SSE events** instead of adding fields to ChatStreamChunk.
 Extensions add new event variants through the SSEChatStreamEventVariants enum using
 the compose_chat_stream_events macro.

 Example: The title extension sends a separate `TitleUpdated` event instead of
 adding a title field to ChatStreamChunk.

 Events are sent with proper `event:` names (e.g., "started", "content", "complete", "error", "titleUpdated")
 for type-safe client-side handling.

oneOf.description: Streaming started event (sent before content with message IDs)
Content chunk event (streamed content deltas)
Stream completion event
Error event

SSEChatStreamMcpApprovalRequiredData.description: Event data for MCP tool approval required

server.description: MCP server requesting tool execution (display name)

tool_name.description: Name of the tool requiring approval

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool use requiring approval

SSEChatStreamMcpElicitationRequiredData.description: Event data for MCP elicitation required (server is requesting human input)

message.description: Human-readable prompt from the MCP server

message_id.description: ID of the assistant message that triggered this elicitation (informational)

requested_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the requested fields (per SEP-1330)

server.description: Display name of the MCP server that sent the request

SSEChatStreamMcpToolCompleteData.description: Event data for MCP tool execution completion

is_error.description: Whether the tool execution resulted in an error

result.description: Tool result text, truncated to 2000 chars (for display in "Show details" panel)

server.description: MCP server that executed the tool

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool use

SSEChatStreamMcpToolProgressData.description:
Event data for MCP tool progress (server sent a `notifications/progress`
 during a long-running tool call — e.g. a sandbox rootfs download).

message_id.description: ID of the assistant message whose tool call is reporting progress.

progress.description: Current progress value (monotonically increasing per the MCP spec).

progress_token.description:
Opaque progress token the server echoed back (the client sets it on
 the originating `tools/call`). Lets the UI correlate concurrent calls.

server.description: Display name of the MCP server reporting progress.

total.description: Total expected units, if known (denominator for a progress bar).

SSEChatStreamMcpToolStartData.description: Event data for MCP tool execution start

input.description: Tool input parameters (for display in "Show details" panel)

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being executed

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool use

SSEChatStreamRunJsApprovalRequiredData.description:
Event data for a `run_js` script's per-call tool approval.

 Emitted while a `run_js` script is SUSPENDED in-process awaiting the user's
 decision on a gated sub-tool call. Unlike `McpApprovalRequired` (a
 turn-boundary flow resumed by re-sending the message), this is resolved by a
 SIDE-CHANNEL `POST /api/mcp/elicitation/{elicitation_id}/respond` — the same
 in-process oneshot mechanism `ask_user` uses — because a live QuickJS call
 stack cannot survive an HTTP-request boundary. `accept` → the sub-tool runs;
 `decline`/`cancel` → the host fn throws a catchable error into the script.

server.description: MCP server that owns the sub-tool (display name)

tool_name.description: Name of the sub-tool the script wants to call

SSEChatStreamStartedData.description:
Data for the Started SSE event
 Sent before content streaming begins to communicate conversation context
 Client learns assistant_message_id from content chunks (message_id field)

user_message_id.description: User message ID (None if resuming with tool approvals or regenerating)

SSEChatStreamTitleUpdatedData.description: Data for the TitleUpdated SSE event

data.description:
D2: rendered seed data the FE form pre-fills with (see
 `StepConfig::Elicit.data`). `None` when the step declares no `data:`.

total_bytes.description:
May be `None` when the upstream omits Content-Length (rare
 for GitHub Releases). The UI shows an indeterminate bar.

SSELogLagData.description:
Emitted when the broadcast buffer overflowed and the subscriber
 missed lines (slow reader).

SSELogLineData.description: A single captured engine log line.

step_manifest.description:
The full pipeline manifest for live first-paint (descriptions rendered
 against inputs). Mirrors `SSESnapshotData.step_manifest`.

error.description:
The run's terminal error, if any. Carried on the snapshot so a client
 that subscribes AFTER the run already failed (never receiving the live
 `RunFailed` event) still renders the run-level error alert.

step_manifest.description:
The full pipeline (topo order) so a (re)connecting client renders all
 steps up front — pending ones included. Rebuilt from the run's
 compiled IR. Empty for legacy/in-flight rows without a manifest.

step_progress_json.description:
P2.6: the running sandbox step's live track map (`{ id -> ProgressTrack }`)
 so a (re)connecting client rehydrates in-flight bars. `None` when no step
 is currently streaming progress.

SSEStepManifestItem.description:
One step in the pipeline manifest the FE renders up front (Part 1, D4
 Option B). `description` is rendered against inputs (best-effort) for
 pending steps; the FE upgrades it to the full-context render on
 `StepStarted`.

tracks.description: The tracks that changed in this throttle flush (P2.5 coalesce → batch).

description.description:
Full-context render of the step's `description` (inputs + completed
 step outputs). The FE upgrades the manifest row's label to this.

SandboxAvailability.description:
Why `code_sandbox` is (or isn't) initialized, in machine-readable form.

 `Ready` means `init()` reached the end and `get_state()` is `Some`. Every
 other variant is a specific early-return reason recorded by `init()`; the
 rootfs-versions admin endpoint surfaces it so the UI can degrade gracefully
 (show the GitHub catalog + a precise notice) instead of a blanket error.

oneOf.description: Fully initialized — the sandbox is registered and usable.
`code_sandbox.enabled: false` in config (the default).
The host boot probe failed — on Linux this means `bwrap` is not on PATH.

The cloud instance-metadata endpoint is reachable and
 `allow_cloud_imds_reachable` is not set, so registration was refused.
The per-conversation workspace root could not be created.
State exists but carries no DB pool (an in-process test edge case).
`init()` has not run yet, or bailed before recording a reason.

SandboxFlavorsResponse.description:
REST response for the MCP-server form's sandbox flavor picker:
 the selectable rootfs flavors plus the host command allowlist.

host_allowed_commands.description:
Commands allowed for a NON-sandboxed (host) stdio MCP server. A
 sandboxed server (run_in_sandbox) may use any command.

SaveUserApiKeyRequest.description: Request to save a user API key

SaveUserConnectorKeyRequest.description: PUT body to set the calling user's own key for a connector.

SaveUserProviderKeyRequest.description: PUT body to set the calling user's own key for a provider.

paused_reason.description:
Set when AUTO-paused (`max_failures` / `conversation_deleted` /
 `target_missing`); NULL for a user enable/disable.

ScheduledTaskRun.description: A row of `scheduled_task_runs` — one per firing (the "Runs" history).

SendMessageRequest.description:
Request to send a message in a conversation

 Extensions contribute fields by creating an extension.rs file with a RequestFields struct.
 The build script automatically discovers and registers these extensions.

assistant_id.description:
Optional assistant ID to use for this message.
 The assistant's instructions will be injected as a system message.

branch_id.description:
Branch ID to send message to (required)
 This branch will be used as the parent when creating a new branch

create_branch_from_message_id.description:
If set, creates a new branch from this message before sending
 The message must belong to the specified branch_id
 The new branch becomes the active branch

enable_mcp.description: Enable MCP tool calling for this request

file_ids.description: Array of file IDs to attach to the message

fork_level.description:
Fork level for the new branch: 'user' (edit flow) or 'assistant' (regenerate flow).
 Only used when create_branch_from_message_id is set. Defaults to 'user'.

mcp_config.description: MCP configuration specifying which servers and tools to enable

model_id.description: Model ID to use for this message (required)

tool_approvals.description: Tool approval decisions for resuming after approval workflow

SendMessageResponse.description:
Response to a fire-and-forget send: the persisted message ids. The assistant
 reply arrives as live frames over `GET /api/chat/stream` (tokens), and the
 finished turn lands via `sync:conversation` + refetch.

user_message_id.description: None if an extension suppressed the user message (e.g. tool-approval resume).

SessionSettings.description: Deployment-wide JWT session settings (singleton row). Returned by GET.

refresh_token_expiry_days.description:
Max session length in days (1..=3650) — the refresh-token TTL.
 Active sessions roll on every refresh, so this is the idle bound.

SetAdminPasswordRequest.description:
POST /api/remote-access/admin-password request.

 No `current_password` field — physical presence at the desktop
 (proven by the localhost-Host middleware) IS the auth proof.
 The standard `/api/users/me/password` flow that requires the
 current password is still available for multi-user web deployments;
 this endpoint exists specifically for the single-admin desktop
 case where the bootstrap default is a published string and
 requiring it would be friction without security benefit.

SetMcpServerOAuthConfigRequest.description: Request to set (create or replace) a server's OAuth config.

SetSubscriptionRequest.description:
Body of `PUT /api/chat/stream/subscription`: the conversation whose live
 tokens this connection wants (or `null` to receive nothing).

timeout_secs.description:
New wall-clock cap in seconds; `0` = unbounded (no timeout). Named to
 match the `timeout_secs` used across the rest of the timeout surface.

SetupStatusResponse.description:
Setup-status response shown to unauthenticated callers.

 Closes 13-misc F-02 (Medium): the original response leaked the
 app name + version to every anonymous fetch, which is fingerprint
 material attackers use to pick a known-CVE matrix. We now expose
 only the single bit the client actually needs (`needs_setup`).
 app_name / version remain available to authenticated callers via
 a separate endpoint if needed.

Skill.description:
Database row in `skills`. The bundle's SKILL.md + reference files
 live on disk at `extracted_path`; the row carries metadata only.

description.description:
SKILL.md frontmatter `description` — Path-B listing line the
 model sees in the system prompt.

bundle_sha256.description: Hex-encoded sha256 of the original tar.gz; used for re-verification.

entry_point.description: Conventional entry-point file inside the bundle (`"SKILL.md"`).

extracted_path.description: Absolute path on disk to the extracted bundle dir.

frontmatter_json.description:
FULL parsed YAML frontmatter — JSONB. Opaque-preserving so unknown
 fields (`allowed-tools`, `disable-model-invocation`, `paths`, etc.)
 round-trip if the bundle is re-exported.

is_dev.description:
True when imported via `/api/skills/import` (dev workflow:
 mock: honored, no version bumping).

name.description: Reverse-DNS canonical name (matches hub identity).

SkillBodyResponse.description:
`GET /api/skills/{id}/body` response — the SKILL.md markdown body
 (frontmatter stripped), read from the extracted bundle on disk. Lets
 the FE SkillDetailDrawer render the real procedural content, not just
 the frontmatter-derived metadata (plan §5 "renders SKILL.md").

SkillListQuery.description:
Optional pagination for the skill listings. Defaults bound an
 un-paginated caller to the first `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE` skills instead of
 returning an unbounded set.

SkillListResponse.description:
`GET /api/skills` response shape — user-owned + accessible system
 skills, each tagged with its `scope`.

SummarizationAdminSettings.description:
Deployment-wide summarization admin settings (singleton row).

 `default_summarization_model_id` is intentionally nullable: when
 NULL, the chat extension falls back to the conversation's own
 model (zero-config). The token thresholds + prompt overrides are
 runtime-tunable knobs for operators with workloads that need a
 different shape than the compiled defaults.

full_summary_prompt.description:
Custom prompt for the full-resume path. NULL → use the compiled-in
 default. Must contain `{transcript}` placeholder when set.

incremental_summary_prompt.description:
Custom prompt for the incremental-fold path. NULL → use the
 compiled-in default. Must contain `{previous_summary}` AND
 `{new_transcript}` placeholders when set.

SwapOutcome.description:
Result of a pin change. Surfaced via the `set-pin` HTTP handler so
 the admin UI can render a "n session(s) draining" indicator.

oneOf.description: Same major version → workspace data is preserved verbatim.

Different majors → wipe install-cache subdirs in every
 conversation- + MCP-server-workspace AFTER drain.

SwapRuntimeVersionRequest.description: Request to swap a model onto another version of the **same** engine.

restarted.description: True if a running instance was restarted onto the new version.

SyncCacheResponse.description: Response after syncing cache with database

SyncConnectedData.description:
Handshake payload: the server-assigned connection id. The client
 echoes it back via the `X-Sync-Connection-Id` header on mutations so
 the fan-out can skip the originating connection (self-echo suppression).

SyncEntity.description:
The kind of entity that changed. Serialized snake_case to match the frontend's `sync:` event vocabulary. ADD a variant here when wiring a new domain. NOTE: there is no central `audience_kind` match — each emitting handler picks the `Audience` explicitly at the `publish` call site (`Audience::owner(..)` / `Audience::perm::

()` / `Audience::everyone()`). So adding a variant does NOT force an audience assignment at compile time; the author must choose the correct audience at every emit site for the new entity (an owner-scoped entity broadcast to everyone would be a leak). Keep new entities' audiences aligned with the read-permission gating their refetch endpoint enforces.

oneOf.description:
A user-created assistant (NOT a system template — that's a separate
 entity with a different audience).
A user-owned MCP server (NOT a system/group-shared server).

A user's own profile (display fields / active state). Emitted to the
 affected user — e.g. when an admin edits their account — so their
 other devices re-bootstrap `/auth/me`.

A user's saved LLM-provider API key (`id` is the provider id; only
 masked state is ever exposed, and only via refetch).

A user's saved web_search provider key changed (set/clear). Owner-scoped;
 notify-only — the client refetches `GET /api/web-search/user-keys` (masked
 state only). `id` is `Uuid::nil()` (the key is addressed by provider name,
 not a uuid; the refetch reloads the whole per-user catalog).

A user's saved lit_search connector key changed (set/clear). Owner-scoped;
 notify-only — the client refetches `GET /api/lit-search/user-keys`.

A chat conversation owned by the user. `id` is the conversation id.
 Emitted on create/rename/delete, on each completed message turn, and on
 branch/message edits — the recipient refetches the list and (if open)
 the conversation's messages. Live assistant TOKENS do NOT ride this
 stream; they go over the dedicated `chat/stream` token channel.

A user-owned file whose version set / head changed — via a REST restore,
 the built-in `files_mcp` write tools (create_file / edit_file /
 edit_file_lines / rewrite_file), or a code-sandbox version-back. `id` is
 the stable file_id; the recipient refetches the file + its versions (if
 open in a panel).

A recorded MCP tool-call invocation (`mcp_tool_calls`). Emitted
 owner-scoped on Create from the detached recording task in
 `McpSession::call_tool`, so the per-server "Calls" tab refreshes live.
 Notify-only; the client refetches via `GET /api/mcp/tool-calls`.

The caller's own default MCP settings for new conversations changed
 (approval mode / auto-approved tools / disabled servers / loop
 settings). Owner-scoped; notify-only — the client refetches
 `GET /api/mcp/defaults` (gated `conversations::read`). `id` is
 `Uuid::nil()` (a per-user singleton addressed by owner, not a uuid).
Admin view of an LLM provider (full admin provider table).
Admin view of an LLM model.
A user group (admin tables; includes group permissions).
A user account (admin users list).

A shared assistant template (visible to any user who can read
 templates — non-secret, uniform view).
Admin view of a system (deployment-shared) MCP server.
An LLM repository (admin).
A local-runtime engine version (admin).
Deployment-wide local-runtime engine settings (singleton).
Deployment-wide memory admin settings (singleton).
Deployment-wide Document-RAG (file_rag) admin settings (singleton).
A user's Letta-style assistant core-memory blocks changed (owner-scoped).
Code-sandbox resource-limit settings (singleton).
run_js (js_tool) resource-limit settings (singleton, admin-scoped).
Code-sandbox rootfs version list changed (install/evict/delete).
Hub catalog settings (singleton).

Admin view of an authentication provider (Google/Microsoft/Apple/LDAP
 /OIDC). Emitted on create/update/test/delete and on auto-disable. The
 public `/api/auth/providers` (login page) is unaffected — it just shows
 the next list state on the next page load.

Deployment-wide summarization settings (singleton). Notify-only;
 the frontend refetches via the existing REST endpoint.

Deployment-wide JWT session settings (singleton): access-token TTL +
 max session length. Notify-only; the admin UI refetches via
 `GET /api/auth/session-settings`.

Deployment-wide web search settings + provider config (singleton).
 Notify-only; the frontend refetches settings + the provider catalog.

Deployment-wide literature search settings + connector config (singleton).
 Notify-only; the frontend refetches settings + the connector catalog.

Deployment-wide MCP user policy (singleton `mcp_user_policy`): which
 transports regular users may install + the enforced stdio sandbox
 flavor + tool-call retention. Delivered to holders of `mcp_servers::read`
 (the read-perm gating `GET /api/mcp/user-policy`); notify-only — each
 recipient refetches the sanitized policy. `id` is `Uuid::nil()`.

Deployment-wide scheduler admin settings (singleton): per-user task
 quota, cadence floor, failure cap, notification retention. Delivered to
 holders of `scheduler::admin::read`; notify-only — the admin UI refetches
 `GET /api/scheduler/admin-settings`. `id` is `Uuid::nil()`.

A user's bibliography library entry changed (add/import/verify/delete).
 Owner-scoped; notify-only — the client refetches `/api/citations`.

A user's scheduled task changed (create/update/enable/pause/delete) or a
 firing advanced its run history. Owner-scoped; notify-only — the client
 refetches `/api/scheduled-tasks` (and, if open, the task's runs).

A new (or updated/read) notification for the user. Owner-scoped;
 emitted with origin=None from the background firing so every device
 refetches `/api/notifications` (+ the unread count). `id` is the
 notification id (or `Uuid::nil()` for a bulk read-all/prune).
A user's accessible-providers (with enabled models) view changed.
A user's accessible (system) MCP-servers view changed.

The user's session/permissions changed (group membership or a group's
 permissions were edited) — the client re-bootstraps `/auth/me`. `id`
 is the affected user id.

A user-installed skill (user-scope). Notify-only — the client refetches
 `/api/skills` (which returns the user's own + accessible system skills).

Admin view of a system-scope skill (assigned via group_skills).
 Emitted ALONGSIDE `Skill` when scope='system' to refresh both surfaces.
A user-installed workflow (user-scope). Same shape as Skill.
Admin view of a system-scope workflow.

A workflow_runs lifecycle transition (started / completed / failed /
 cancelled) — NOT per-step events; those go on the dedicated per-run
 SSE channel (§4.4). Notify-only so cross-device list views refresh.

The caller's onboarding progress changed (a guide / step completed).
 Owner-scoped, notify-only — other devices refetch `/api/onboarding/me`
 so a guide completed on one device doesn't keep showing on another.

SyncEvent.description: The change notification pushed to clients. Notify-and-refetch only.

TestFireRequest.description: The target config to test (an unsaved drawer config or a saved task's fields).

TestFireResult.description: The inline result of a test-fire.

TestMcpConnectionRequest.description:
Request to test an MCP server connection without persisting anything.

 Carries the same transport fields as a create/update request so the UI can
 probe the *current form values* before saving. `oauth` is the credentials
 typed into the form (new external server); since the client secret is
 write-only in the edit / list flows, `id` lets the server fall back to the
 stored OAuth config for that existing server.

environment_variables_entries.description:
Same structured shape as create/update — for entries the user
 hasn't touched (saved-secret entries with `value: None`), the
 test path falls back to the decrypted stored value via `id`
 (mirrors the existing OAuth-secret fallback comment below).

id.description:
Existing server id — used ONLY to recover the stored OAuth secret when
 `oauth` is absent (edit drawer / list card). Access-checked before use.

oauth.description: OAuth client_credentials typed into the form (new external HTTP server).

TestMcpConnectionResponse.description:
Result of a connection test — `success` is the only authoritative field.
 On failure `message` carries the underlying error (timeout / 401 / bad
 command). On success `tool_count` is the number of tools the server
 advertised (best-effort; `None` if the handshake succeeded but listing
 tools failed).

conversation_id.description:
Conversation to snapshot the model from (mirrors /run). Required
 for `real_llm` fixtures; ci fixtures are fully mocked but still
 need a model snapshot to build the (never-called) provider object.

redacted_data.description: Opaque data for a redacted-thinking block (Anthropic).

signature.description:
Anthropic extended-thinking signature — required to replay the block on a
 later turn (`None` for Gemini/OpenAI, whose thinking isn't replayable).

ToolIdentifier.description: Identifies a specific tool on a specific server

input.description: Tool input (serialized as "input" for API compatibility)

status.description: Approval status (stored as VARCHAR in DB, serialized as String for API)

TriggerResponse.description: Response for the fire-and-forget admin triggers.

UpdateAssistantRequest.description: Request structure for updating an existing assistant

is_default.description:
Update default status
 Setting to true will unset other defaults in the same context

name.description: Update assistant name

parameters.description: Update model parameters

config.description:
Patch the provider's JSONB config. Empty `client_secret`
 field is treated as "leave the existing value unchanged" so
 admins can edit other fields without re-entering secrets.

UpdateCodeSandboxResourceLimits.description:
Admin PUT payload. All fields optional; absent fields preserve their
 existing value. Mirrors the `llm_provider` admin-update pattern.

UpdateConnectorRequest.description:
Update one connector's config + key. Tri-state key: absent = leave, empty
 string = clear, value = set.

api_key.description: API key: omitted = leave, "" = clear, non-empty = set.

memory_mode.description:
One of `"inherit"`, `"on"`, `"off"`. Setting `"inherit"`
 deletes any stored override (row absence == inherit).

title.description: Title update: None = don't update, Some(None) = clear to null, Some(Some(value)) = set value

UpdateFileRagAdminSettingsRequest.description:
Partial update body for `PUT /api/file-rag/admin-settings`.

 `embedding_model_id` uses the `Option<Option<Uuid>>` pattern: absent =
 unchanged, `null` = clear, value = set (see `deserialize_nullable_field`).
 `embedding_dimensions` is intentionally NOT a field — the handler derives
 it by probe-embedding the chosen model. `fts_dictionary` is intentionally
 NOT a field in v1 (kept at `'simple'` to match the GENERATED column).

UpdateGroupSystemServersRequest.description: Request to update system MCP servers for a group

UpdateGroupSystemSkillsRequest.description:
`PUT /api/groups/{group_id}/system-skills` body — the full desired set
 of system-skill ids for the group. Mirrors MCP's
 `UpdateGroupSystemServersRequest`.

UpdateGroupSystemWorkflowsRequest.description:
`PUT /api/groups/{group_id}/system-workflows` body — the full desired set
 of system-workflow ids for the group. Mirrors MCP's
 `UpdateGroupSystemServersRequest`.

UpdateHostMountPolicyRequest.description: Deployment policy — PUT body (tri-state-free: omitted field = unchanged).

UpdateJsToolSettings.description:
Admin PUT payload. All fields optional; absent fields preserve their existing
 value (COALESCE PATCH). Mirrors `UpdateCodeSandboxResourceLimits`.

UpdateLitSearchSettingsRequest.description:
Partial update for `PUT /api/lit-search/settings`. Every field optional →
 absent = leave (matches the web_search peer's DTO shape).

UpdateLlmModelRequest.description: Request to update an existing LLM model

required_runtime_version_id.description: Optional runtime version to pin this model to

environment_variables_entries.description:
Replaces the existing env vars wholesale when present.
 `None` means "don't touch" (caller didn't include this field).
 `Some(empty vec)` clears all entries. Per-entry `value: None`
 keeps the existing secret value (see `EnvVarEntry`).

run_in_sandbox.description:
Launch the stdio subprocess inside the code_sandbox bwrap
 isolation. Same force-set semantics as
 [`CreateMcpServerRequest::run_in_sandbox`].

sandbox_flavor.description:
Rootfs flavor (KNOWN_FLAVORS, e.g. "minimal" / "full") for the
 sandboxed launch. Same force-override semantics as
 [`CreateMcpServerRequest::sandbox_flavor`].

UpdateMcpUserPolicyRequest.description:
PUT body. `updated_by` is taken from the auth context, not the
 request.

tool_call_retention_days.description:
Days to retain MCP tool-call history; `0` = keep forever. Clamped to
 [0, 3650] on save. Omitted (`None`) keeps the current value, so
 existing PUT callers that don't set it are unaffected.

UpdateMemoryAdminSettingsRequest.description:
Admin settings update body.

 The `Option<Option<T>>` pattern on nullable columns means: outer
 `None` = leave the field unchanged, `Some(None)` = clear to NULL
 (use the compiled-in default for prompts, or "no default" for
 embedding/extraction models), `Some(Some(x))` = set to `x`.

 Serde's default `Option<T>` deserialization collapses `null` and
 "absent" to the same `None`, so the discriminating "Some(None)"
 state requires a custom deserializer — see
 `deserialize_nullable_field` below.

UpdateProfileRequest.description:
Self-service profile update for the authenticated user. Only the
 safe fields are accepted here: `email` is intentionally NOT
 editable (re-verification flow not built; was removed to close an
 OAuth account-takeover vector) and `is_active`/`is_admin`/
 `permissions` are admin-only and can never be set through this path.

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Request to update an existing project. All fields optional.

default_assistant_id.description:
Tri-state on FKs (missing = no change; null = clear; uuid = set).
 The frontend uses the existing deserialize_nullable_field helper
 from the chat module for symmetry.

UpdateProviderRequest.description:
PUT body for one provider's config/key.
 `api_key`: absent = leave; `""` = clear; non-empty = set.
 `config`: absent = leave; present = replace.

UpdateRemoteAccessSettingsRequest.description:
PUT /api/remote-access/settings request body. Each field uses
 three-state semantics:
   - missing key → don't touch (keep DB value)
   - null → clear (set to NULL / FALSE)
   - value → set

 Implemented via `Option<Option<T>>` + a custom serde fn that
 distinguishes "absent" from "null". The frontend matches by
 only sending fields it actually wants to change.

auto_start_tunnel.description: Booleans don't need null semantics — absent means "don't touch".

UpdateScheduledTask.description: Update-task request body (all fields optional; only present ones change).

UpdateSchedulerAdminSettings.description: Admin update body (all fields required — the form always sends the full set).

UpdateSessionSettingsRequest.description: PUT body for the session settings. Every field optional → absent = leave.

checked_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last successful check, or null if never.

enabled.description:
Whether update checks are enabled in config (false → air-gapped or the
 embedded desktop server).

latest_version.description: Latest version seen on GitHub, or null if not yet checked / disabled.

notes.description: Release notes (markdown) for the latest version.

release_url.description: GitHub release page for the latest version.

UpdateSummarizationAdminSettingsRequest.description:
Partial-update request for the singleton admin settings row.

 The four nullable fields (model id + the two prompts) use the
 `Option<Option<T>>` tri-state:
   missing  → `None`        → leave the column alone
   `null`   → `Some(None)`  → clear the column
   value    → `Some(Some(v))` → set the column

UpdateWebSearchSettingsRequest.description: PUT body for the global settings. Every field optional → absent = leave.

auto_approved_tools.description:
Auto-approved tools grouped by server
 Format: [{"server_id": "uuid", "tools": ["tool1", "tool2"]}, ...]
 None = preserve existing value in DB; Some(vec) = overwrite with this value

disabled_servers.description:
Disabled servers/tools (empty = all servers enabled)
 Format: [{"server_id": "uuid", "tools": []}, ...] (empty tools = entire server disabled)

loop_settings.description: Loop settings for controlling iteration behavior

UpsertUserMcpDefaultsRequest.description: Request to create/update user MCP defaults

auto_approved_tools.description:
Auto-approved tools grouped by server
 None = preserve existing value in DB; Some(vec) = overwrite with this value

loop_settings.description: Loop settings for controlling iteration behavior

cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Prompt tokens written to cache this request.

cache_read_input_tokens.description: Prompt tokens served from cache (the prompt-cache hit signal).

reasoning_tokens.description: Reasoning/thinking tokens (if the provider reports them).

password_changed_at.description:
When the user last rotated their password. NULL means the
 account is still using the bootstrap-issued password (only
 meaningful for the desktop `admin` user). The Remote Access
 module refuses to enable password authentication unless this
 is non-NULL for the admin.

UserConnectorKeyCatalogEntry.description:
One row in the user-facing key catalog: a key-accepting connector joined with
 the calling user's own key state + whether a deployment (shared) key exists.
 Neither the user key nor the deployment key value is ever exposed.

key_field.description:
The connector's key-field descriptor (label / help / docs / required),
 so the user form renders the same guidance as the admin surface.

system_key_set.description:
True when the deployment/admin has a shared key for this connector — the
 fallback used when the user sets none. Boolean only, never the value.

user_key.description: The user's own key in masked form, or `null` when they've set none.

UserMcpDefaultsResponse.description: User MCP defaults (API response - properly typed)

loop_settings.description: Loop settings for controlling iteration behavior

UserMemory.description:
A persisted user-level memory row.

 `embedding` is intentionally omitted from the public response shape —
 embeddings are internal retrieval machinery, never user-facing.

UserProviderKeyCatalogEntry.description:
One row in the user-facing key catalog: a key-accepting provider joined with
 the calling user's own key state + whether a deployment (shared) key exists.
 Neither the user key nor the deployment key value is ever exposed.

system_key_set.description:
True when the deployment/admin has a shared key for this provider — the
 fallback used when the user sets none. Boolean only, never the value.

user_key.description: The user's own key in masked form, or `null` when they've set none.

UserProvidersQuery.description:
Optional offset pagination for the user provider list. Defaults bound an
 un-paginated caller to the first `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE` providers.

location.description:
Source location of the error (e.g. a frontmatter field). Named
 `location` to agree with the workflow validate surface.

ValidateWorkflowRequest.description:
JSON body for `/validate` — just the workflow.yaml text. (A multipart
 tarball would also work, but validate only needs the entry-point YAML
 since it never installs.)

VerificationStatus.description:
Verification outcome for a stored or checked reference.

 `not_found` is reserved for a **supplied identifier that fails to resolve**
 (the fabricated-DOI case). An entry with no identifier at all rests at
 `unverified` — absence of an id is NOT a red flag (books, theses, grey
 literature, datasets, in-press all legitimately lack a DOI/PMID).

VerifyCitationsRequest.description: Verify a reference list without persisting (the REST analogue of `verify_citations`).

VersionStatus.description:
Snapshot returned by `status()` for the admin UI's "Rootfs
 versions" page.

availability.description:
Whether `code_sandbox` is initialized, and if not, the machine-readable
 reason. `ready` when the sandbox is registered (full status); otherwise a
 degraded snapshot — the GitHub `available` catalog with empty
 `installed`/`pinned` — so the admin UI can explain WHY installing/mounting
 is unavailable instead of showing a blanket error.

available.description: Only populated when GitHub is reachable (best-effort).

conversation_count.description:
Count of per-conversation workspace dirs that would be
 touched by a major-bump wipe. Read by the major-bump confirm
 modal so the admin sees blast radius before clicking through.

draining.description:
One entry per live mount (registered after a successful
 `runtime_mount::ensure_rootfs_ready`). The admin UI uses
 this to render per-row "in-flight" counts + draining
 indicators on rows being phased out by a set-pin.

host_arch.description:
The local host's CPU arch (`"x86_64"` | `"aarch64"`). Authoritative —
 the admin UI uses this to offer the artifact this machine can run,
 instead of guessing from installed artifacts (which is empty on a fresh
 host).

host_package.description:
The rootfs package format the local backend can actually mount
 (`"squashfs"` on Linux/macOS, `"tar.zst"` on Windows/WSL2). Authoritative
 — prevents a fresh Windows host from pre-fetching an unmountable squashfs.

mcp_server_workspace_count.description:
Same as above, for per-MCP-server workspaces under
 `<workspace_root>/mcp/<server_id>/`.

VersionUsageEntry.description: One installed engine version + the models that effectively resolve to it.

VersionUsageResponse.description: Models grouped by the engine version they effectively run on.

unresolved.description: Local models whose engine has no installed version to resolve to.

WebSearchSettings.description: Deployment-wide web search settings (singleton row). Returned by GET.

provider_chain.description: Ordered fallback chain of provider registry keys.

compiled_ir_json.description:
Pre-resolved templates + typed step metadata. NULL until the
 validator's compile pass runs (B4). See plan §4.1 pattern (d).

conversation_id.description:
The owning conversation for an `ephemeral` row (else NULL). Set so the
 row (and its runs) GC when the conversation is deleted.

ephemeral.description:
An LLM-authored, conversation-scoped throwaway workflow materialized by
 the `run_from_workspace` verb. Excluded from every listing (never a
 `wf_<slug>` tool nor on the workflows page) — it only runs via the
 generic verb. CASCADE-cleaned with `conversation_id`.

WorkflowListQuery.description:
Optional pagination for the workflow listing. Defaults bound an
 un-paginated caller to the first `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE` workflows instead of
 returning an unbounded set.

WorkflowRun.description:
Database row in `workflow_runs`. Heavy fields (step outputs, logs,
 artifacts, final output) live as JSONB metadata blobs — actual
 content (multi-MiB step output, artifact bytes) is on disk under
 the per-run workspace.

step_progress_json.description:
P2.6: the running sandbox step's live progress track map
 (`{ id -> ProgressTrack }`), or NULL when no step is streaming progress.
 Drives Snapshot rehydration of in-flight bars after a refresh.

capture_logs.description:
Opt-in: force full per-step log capture (prompt / raw_output / stderr /
 items) for THIS run regardless of the workflow's per-step `log:` levels,
 so a manually-launched run is debuggable in the run history. Off by
 default. The captured logs are surfaced to the run owner only.

mocks.description:
Per-step canned responses, keyed by step id. ONLY honored when the
 workflow's `is_dev = true` (the route handler rejects mocks for
 published workflows with 403). Lets dev iteration + `tests/` fixtures
 stub specific steps without spending LLM tokens. See plan §1.

model_id.description:
Explicit model for a standalone (no-conversation) run, picked in the
 Run dialog. When set, it wins over the conversation's model and is
 access-checked against the user's providers (the run handler is only
 gated on `WorkflowsExecute`, so this is the per-model authorization).
 When unset, the model is snapshotted from `conversation_id` (legacy
 path). A run with neither is rejected (`WORKFLOW_NO_MODEL_SOURCE`).

WorkflowRunSummary.description:
Lightweight run-history row (A4) — excludes the heavy JSONB blobs the full
 `WorkflowRun` carries; backs `GET /workflows/{id}/runs`.

WorkspaceSaveRequest.description:
Promote a workflow the model authored in its sandbox workspace into the
 user's permanent library. `scope="system"` is admin-only (re-checked by
 `resolve_import_scope` inside `install_workflow_from_bytes`).

conversation_id.description: The conversation whose sandbox workspace holds the bundle.

dir.description:
The workspace subdir (relative to the conversation workspace) with
 `workflow.yaml` (+ any `scripts/`).

name.description: Optional slug/name for the saved workflow.
